note: Phase 1 seed covering hadolint's DL3000/DL4000 series (Dockerfile correctness + idiom). SC-prefixed rules are passed through from embedded ShellCheck and are already covered by shared/rules/languages/shell.json — not re-mapped here. Security-framed Docker concerns (hardcoded secrets, privileged FROM, curl|sh patterns) are NOT hadolint's remit; Hydra R3 owns container-image CWE taxonomy. DL3002 (final user is root) and DL3004 (sudo usage) are defense-in-depth posture signals that sit adjacent to security — routed to security_defer_to_hydra to preserve the non-duplication contract with Hydra.

correctness_m1.description: Rules flagging Dockerfile runtime-failure candidates: invalid instruction combinations, unreachable stage references, unpinned dependencies that will break reproducibility. M1 widens on COPY/FROM graph; M5 cannot witness container-build at zero-dep, so these stay at static confidence.

idiom_m7.description: Dockerfile idiom — prefer the canonical construct over the hand-rolled one (ADD vs COPY, JSON-form CMD, --no-cache variants). Routed to M7 (Idiom-fit).

complexity_m7.description: Layer/step complexity — consolidating RUN layers, avoiding commands unsuitable for image builds. Routed to M7 (Simplicity).

naming_m7.description: Dockerfile label and alias naming conventions. Routed to M7 (Clarity).

testability_m7.description: Phase 2 gap — hadolint does not flag testability concerns for Dockerfiles directly. Seeded empty; revisit when hadolint emits image-scan-friendly checks.

security_defer_to_hydra.description: Posture rules adjacent to container security (root user, sudo escalation). Hydra R3 owns CWE-250 / CWE-269 / image-layer CVE surfaces. Lich records the hadolint fire but does NOT emit a finding — boosts M6 attention weight on the Dockerfile instead.
